banner.title: AFTER THE GAME
banner.description: A new initiative for transitioning athletes.

atg.initiative: A BEYOND CODE COLLECTIVE ANCHOR PROGRAM
atg.description: After The Game is a national, cohort-based workforce program for transitioning professional and NCAA student-athletes. It is explicitly designed for those who are not long-term earners and need structured support to design and enter a second career. This structured program bridges the “digital skill divide” with technical leaders teaching live classes and building curricula, and a community providing wraparound support that covers everything from corporate readiness to job placement.
atg.videoDescription: Hear directly from athletes who are building their next chapter through After The Game. The transition doesn't have to be a solo journey.
atg.problemText: Less than 2 percent of NCAA student-athletes go on to play professionally, and for those who do, the clock is already ticking. NFL careers last an average of 3–5 years; NBA careers, 4.5 years; and MLB, 5 years. When it comes to deciding what’s next, most athletes are left to figure it out alone.
atg.problemText2: This is not because they lack discipline, motivation or drive. What they lack is a bridge: the knowledge, context, and guidance to translate their skills into the next career they want.
programItems: Early, advisory coaching to clarify direction
A shared workforce and digital foundations core
atg.classroomText: After The Game uses a “flipped-classroom” model using peer-based milestones and hands-on coding labs that respect the athlete’s learning style: facilitators over lecturers and team-based competition. Participants are selected based on proven motivation to build a durable post-sports career, demonstrated through a pre-program interview and selection process.
atg.curriculumText: After The Game is a part-time, 6–9 month program that begins with a skills assessment to determine what technical path is best aligned to the individual to ensure higher likelihood of completion and satisfaction. The curriculum goes beyond technical training, integrating wraparound programming such as workplace fundamentals to support productivity tools literacy (e.g. Zoom, Slack, AI fundamentals), professional communication, financial literacy, and mental health support to navigate the psychological identity shift from “Elite Athlete” to “Tech Innovator.”
atg.ecosystemText: We’re building a two-sided ecosystem, a value loop that delivers workforce-ready talent to employers while providing athletes with a clear roadmap to high-trajectory careers: upon completion of the program, participants will be placed in paid externships and apprenticeships with partner employers for 3–6 months.
atg.formDescription: The next cohort starts summer 2026. Sign up now to reserve your spot and get updates on enrollment.
atg.successMessage: We'll be in touch when enrollment opens.

forge.description: The Forge is a network of intergenerational, site-specific hubs that blend technology education, community engagement, and workforce development under one roof. A true “third place” that is neither school nor work, where middle schoolers sit alongside mid-career professionals, and families with their kids, as they explore AI and emerging technology together.
forge.quoteText: At Beyond Code, we believe that everyone should have a role in shaping tomorrow’s technology. The Forge allows us to not only give people access to tools, but the community support and space to explore these tools, so they are empowered to confidently shape the future of technology and work.
forge.whatText: The Forge is designed to be a safe space to ask questions without fear or shame. Each Forge center delivers culturally responsive programming to its community, serving K-12 students and adults seeking pathways into tech-driven careers.

pillars.title: Intergenerational
pillars.description: Middle schoolers alongside mid-career professionals and families. Every generation learns together.

pillars.title: Culturally Responsive
pillars.description: Designed in collaboration with local stakeholders. Programming shaped by and for the community it serves.

pillars.title: AI & Emerging Tech
pillars.description: Expanding opportunity in AI, data literacy, automation, and the technologies defining the future of work.

pillars.title: Free & Accessible
pillars.description: Free programming offered on a regular schedule. No barriers to entry, no prerequisites required.

programmingTypes.title: Lunch & Learn
programmingTypes.description: Open, one-time sessions on topics like AI, data literacy, and digital tools. No commitment required — just show up, learn, and connect.

programmingTypes.title: Cohort Programs
programmingTypes.description: Multi-week structured courses like AI Fundamentals. Limited seats, deeper learning, hands-on projects, and a dedicated instructor-led experience.

forge.programmingNote: No preparation needed — you don’t need to bring a computer. Just come with an open mind and ready to learn.

howItWorksSteps.title: Discover
howItWorksSteps.description: Find The Forge through our website, a friend, or community outreach.

howItWorksSteps.title: Browse the Calendar
howItWorksSteps.description: View upcoming Lunch & Learns and cohort programs on our Eventbrite calendar.

howItWorksSteps.title: RSVP
howItWorksSteps.description: Register for free through Eventbrite. Cohort programs have limited seats — sign up early.

howItWorksSteps.title: Show Up & Learn
howItWorksSteps.description: Attend in person at Georgia Tech ATDC. No prep needed, just bring yourself.

forge.atlText: The Forge: ATL is hosted within North Star AI Labs at Georgia Tech’s Advanced Technology Development Center (ATDC). Free programming is offered two days a week, running from March 2026 through August 2026.
forge.atlPartner: In partnership with Georgia Tech’s ATDC
forge.atlSchedule: Two days a week, free programming
forge.atlLocation: North Star AI Labs at Georgia Tech ATDC
forge.openingText: The Forge hosted its opening event on March 9, convening some of Atlanta’s leading educators, technologists, and community leaders.
forge.formDescription: Interested in attending The Forge: ATL, volunteering, or partnering? Sign up and we’ll keep you in the loop.
forge.successMessage: We’ll be in touch with programming details and updates.
